canonical_definition: METEOR is consistently characterized across the provided sources as an automatic metric for evaluating text generation. It is frequently mentioned alongside ROUGE as a common evaluation tool. The papers describe its mechanism as word-based, incorporating features like synonymy, stemming, precision, and recall to assess quality. While its general purpose is generation evaluation, its specific application varies; one prevalent definition describes its use for assessing "generated legal responses against reference answers," while another source frames it as a metric for "translation quality."
expert_removed_reason: metric/method/tool — not a benchmark or LLM ability
papers: LiTEx: A Linguistic Taxonomy of Explanations for Understanding Within-Label Variation in Natural Language Inference
MCIP: ProtectingMCPSafety via Model Contextual Integrity Protocol
VeriLocc: End-to-End Cross-Architecture Register Allocation viaLLM
Viability of Machine Translation for Healthcare in Low-Resourced Languages
iKnow-audio: Integrating Knowledge Graphs with Audio-Language Models
Diverse In-Context Example Selection After Decomposing Programs and Aligned Utterances Improves Semantic Parsing
VisDoM: Multi-DocumentQAwith Visually Rich Elements Using Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation

definitions.text: An automatic text-generation evaluation procedure used here to assess generated legal responses against reference answers.
definitions.paper: Diverse In-Context Example Selection After Decomposing Programs and Aligned Utterances Improves Semantic Parsing
